questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: In which one of the following devices, the light energy is converted into the electrical energy?
questions.solution:
The device that converts light energy into electrical energy is the solar cell. Let's go through each option to understand why:

Option A: Light-emitting diode (LED)

A light-emitting diode converts electrical energy into light energy. When an electric current passes through an LED, it emits light due to the recombination of electrons and holes within the semiconductor material.

Option B: Laser diode

Similarly, a laser diode converts electrical energy into light energy, producing coherent light through stimulated emission when an electric current passes through it.

Option C: Solar cell

A solar cell, also known as a photovoltaic cell, converts light energy into electrical energy. When light photons hit the surface of a solar cell, they excite electrons and create electron-hole pairs. This generates a flow of electric current as these electrons move through the cell.

Option D: Transistor

A transistor is a semiconductor device that can amplify or switch electrical signals. It does not convert light energy into electrical energy.

Therefore, Option C: Solar cell is the correct answer.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following sketches correctly describes a lever of second class?
questions.solution:
CONCEPT:

Lever of Second Class

A lever of the second class has three main parts:

  • The fulcrum is at one end. The fulcrum is the point where the lever pivots or turns.
  • The load (the thing you want to move or lift) is in the middle.
  • The effort (where you push or pull) is at the other end, opposite the fulcrum.

In a second-class lever:

  • The load is always between the fulcrum and where you apply effort.
  • The arm where you apply effort (effort arm) is always longer than the part holding the load (load arm), so you get a mechanical advantage greater than 1. This means it's easier to lift something.
  • Examples of second-class levers are wheelbarrows, nutcrackers, and bottle openers.

EXPLANATION:

When you look at sketches of different levers:

  • A second-class lever should always have:
    • Fulcrum at one end,
    • Load in the middle,
    • And effort at the other end.
  • The correct sketch must show the load between the fulcrum and the effort.

So, the sketch that correctly shows a lever of the second class is Option 2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Which of the following statements with reference to L-Wave or Long Wave generated by an earthquake is/are correct?1.They follow the Earth's circumference
2.They travel at more or less at a constant rate
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Statement 1 is correct and Statement 2 is not.

L-waves (surface waves) travel along the Earth’s surface (i.e. its circumference) but are dispersive, so their speed varies with frequency.

Answer: 1 only.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following conclusions could not be derived from Rutherford's α-particle scattering experiment?
options: Most of the space in the atom is empty
The radius of the atom is about 10<sup>5</sup> times the radius of the nucleus
Electrons move in a circular path of fixed energy called orbits
Nearby all the mass of the atom resides in the nucleus
questions.solution:
Option C cannot be derived from Rutherford's $\alpha$-particle scattering experiment.

Rutherford's experiment led to several conclusions about the structure of the atom:

Option A: Most of the space in the atom is empty. This conclusion was drawn because most of the $\alpha$-particles passed through the gold foil without any deflection, indicating that atoms are mostly empty space.

Option B: The radius of the atom is about $10^5$ times the radius of the nucleus. This was inferred from the fact that very few $\alpha$-particles were deflected back, suggesting a tiny, dense nucleus compared to the overall size of the atom.

Option D: Nearly all the mass of the atom resides in the nucleus. This conclusion was reached because the deflection of some $\alpha$-particles implied a very dense center, which contains most of the atomic mass.

Option C refers to the description of electron orbits, which was developed later by Niels Bohr. Bohr's model introduced the concept of electrons in specific orbits with fixed energies, an idea not covered or implied by Rutherford's scattering experiment.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Which one of the following statements regarding simple pendulum is correct?

Simple pendulum has a time period independent of amplitude:
options: only for small amplitudes because then the net force on its bob is independent of its displacement.
for any amplitude because the net force on the bob is always proportional to its displacement.
for any amplitude because the net force on the bob is independent of its displacement.
only for small amplitudes because then the net force on its bob is proportional to its displacement.
questions.solution:
In order to accurately describe the behavior of a simple pendulum, we need to analyze its physics in detail. A simple pendulum consists of a mass (known as the bob) attached to a string of length $ L $ and fixed at a pivot point. When displaced from its equilibrium position and released, it oscillates back and forth under the influence of gravity.

The restoring force acting on the bob is provided by the component of the gravitational force that acts along the direction of the arc. This force can be expressed as:

$$ F = -mg \sin(\theta) $$

where:

  • F is the restoring force,
  • m is the mass of the bob,
  • g is the acceleration due to gravity,
  • θ is the angular displacement from the vertical.

For small angular displacements (small amplitudes), $ \sin(\theta) $ can be approximated by $ \theta $ (when $ \theta $ is measured in radians). Thus, the restoring force becomes:

$$ F \approx -mg\theta $$

Since $ \theta = \frac{x}{L} $ where $ x $ is the linear displacement and $ L $ is the length of the pendulum, the force can be rewritten as:

$$ F \approx -\frac{mg}{L} x $$

This shows that the restoring force is directly proportional to the displacement, characteristic of simple harmonic motion. The time period $ T $ of a simple pendulum is then given by:

$$ T = 2\pi \sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $$

Note that this time period is independent of amplitude, but the approximation $ \sin(\theta) \approx \theta $ only holds for small amplitudes. Therefore, the correct statement regarding the simple pendulum is:

Option D: only for small amplitudes because then the net force on its bob is proportional to its displacement.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements best defines the concept of heat?
options: The transformation of energy from one form to another
The conversion of energy into mass and vice-versa due to temperature difference
The transfer of energy due to temperature difference
The change in volume of a substance with temperature
questions.solution:
Option 1: The transformation of energy from one form to another.

This describes how energy can change its form, like chemical energy turning into electrical energy in a battery, or potential energy turning into kinetic energy as something falls. While heat can be involved in these transformations, this statement describes energy conversion in general, not specifically what heat is.

Option 2: The conversion of energy into mass and vice-versa due to temperature difference.

This touches on the concept of mass-energy equivalence, often represented by Einstein's famous equation $$E=mc^2$$. However, this isn't how we define heat in everyday thermodynamics. Heat isn't about energy converting into mass.

Option 3: The transfer of energy due to temperature difference.

Bingo! This is the most accurate definition. Heat is energy that flows from a warmer object or region to a cooler one because there's a difference in their temperatures. Think about holding an ice cube – heat flows from your warmer hand to the colder ice cube, making your hand feel cold as it loses energy.

Option 4: The change in volume of a substance with temperature.

This phenomenon is called thermal expansion (or contraction), and it's a result of temperature changes. When a substance heats up, its particles move more vigorously and usually spread out, causing an increase in volume. While related to temperature, it's not the definition of heat itself.

So, the statement that best defines the concept of heat is Option 3: The transfer of energy due to temperature difference.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The power required to lift a mass of 8⋅0 kg up a vertical distance of 4 m in 2 s is (taking acceleration due to gravity as 10 m/s<sup>2</sup>):
questions.solution:
To determine the power required, we need to follow these steps:

Calculate the work done to lift the mass.

Calculate the power using the time in which the work is done.

Step 1: Calculate the Work Done

Work done $ W $ is given by:

$ W = m \cdot g \cdot h $

where:

$ m $ is the mass ($ 8.0 \, \text{kg} $),

$ g $ is the acceleration due to gravity ($ 10 \, \text{m/s}^2 $),

$ h $ is the height ($ 4 \, \text{m} $).

So,

$ W = 8.0 \, \text{kg} \times 10 \, \text{m/s}^2 \times 4 \, \text{m} $

$ W = 320 \, \text{J} $

Step 2: Calculate the Power

Power $ P $ is given by:

$ P = \frac{W}{t} $

where $ t $ is the time ($ 2 \, \text{s} $).

So,

$ P = \frac{320 \, \text{J}}{2 \, \text{s}} $

$ P = 160 \, \text{W} $

Conclusion

The power required to lift the mass is $ 160 \, \text{W} $. Therefore, the correct option is:

Option B: 160 W

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the usual unit of measurement for Air Pressure used in India?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: Millibar. Here's why:

Air pressure is the weight of the atmosphere pressing down on a given area. It's measured as the force per unit area. Here's a breakdown of why the other options are incorrect and why millibars are used:

  • Centimeter (Option A): Centimeters measure length or distance, not pressure.
  • Milligram (Option C): Milligrams measure mass, not pressure.
  • Milliliter (Option D): Milliliters measure volume, not pressure.
  • Millibar (Option B): Millibars are the standard unit of measurement for air pressure in India and many other parts of the world. A millibar is a unit of pressure equal to 1/1000 of a bar.

In India, weather reports and forecasts typically use millibars (mb) to indicate air pressure. Other units of measurement for air pressure exist (like inches of mercury), but millibars are the most common in India.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A rectangle ABCD is kept in front of a concave mirror of focal length f with its corners A and B being, respectively, at distances 2f and 3f from the mirror with AB along the principal axis as shown in the figure. It forms an image A'B'C'D' in front of the mirror. What is the ratio of B'C' to A'D'?
questions.solution:
The Mirror Equation:

When rays emanating from a point meet at another point after reflection or refraction, that point is termed the image of the initial point.

The image is termed real if the rays actually converge at that point.

It is virtual if the rays do not actually meet but appear to diverge from that point when extended backward.

The mirror equation is given by: $\frac{1}{f} = \frac{1}{v} + \frac{1}{u}$

Linear magnification (m) is defined as the ratio of the height of the image ($h'$) to the height of the object ($h$): $m = \frac{h'}{h} = -\frac{v}{u}$

Calculation:

Given rectangle ABCD with AD = BC ---- (1)

Corners A and B are at distances 2f and 3f from the mirror, respectively.

For corner A (u = 2f): $ \frac{1}{f} = \frac{1}{v} + \frac{1}{2f} \implies v = 2f $

Magnification: $ m = \frac{h'}{h} = -\frac{v}{u} = -\frac{2f}{2f} = -1 $ This means the size of the image is equal to the size of the object: $ AD = A'D' ---- (2) $

For corner B (u = 3f): $ \frac{1}{f} = \frac{1}{v} + \frac{1}{3f} \implies v = \frac{3}{2}f $

Magnification: $ m = \frac{h'}{h} = -\frac{v}{u} = -\frac{\frac{3}{2}f}{3f} = -\frac{1}{2} $ This means the size of the image is half the size of the object: $ B'C' = \frac{1}{2}BC ---- (3) $

From equations 1, 2, and 3, the ratio of B'C' to A'D' is: $ \frac{B'C'}{A'D'} = \frac{\frac{1}{2}BC}{AD} = \frac{1}{2} $

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is true?
options: The force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon is greater than the force of gravity of the Moon on the Earth.
The force of gravity of the Moon on the Earth is greater than the force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon.
The force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon and of the Moon on the Earth are equal in magnitude and are in the same direction.
The force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon and of the Moon on the Earth are equal in magnitude but are in opposite directions.
questions.solution:
To determine the correct answer, we need to apply Newton's Third Law of Motion. Newton's Third Law states that for every action, there is an equal and opposite reaction. This means the forces two objects exert on each other are equal in magnitude but opposite in direction.

In the case of the Earth and the Moon, the gravitational force that the Earth exerts on the Moon is equal in magnitude to the gravitational force that the Moon exerts on the Earth. However, these forces are in opposite directions.

Mathematically, the force due to gravity between two masses, $m_1$ (Earth) and $m_2$ (Moon), is given by Newton's Law of Universal Gravitation:

$$ F = G \frac{m_1 m_2}{r^2} $$

where $G$ is the gravitational constant, and $r$ is the distance between the centers of the two masses.

Since the masses and the distance between the centers remain the same, the magnitude of the force is the same for both the Earth and the Moon. Hence, by Newton's Third Law, we know that these forces are also equal in magnitude and opposite in direction.

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option D: The force of gravity of the Earth on the Moon and of the Moon on the Earth are equal in magnitude but are in opposite directions.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: If the length of a copper wire is increased by twice, then its resistivity will be
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 3: Same.

• Resistivity (ρ) is an intrinsic property of the material—it doesn’t depend on the wire’s length or cross-section.

• Only the resistance

$$R = \rho\,\frac{L}{A}$$

changes when you alter length (L) or area (A).

• Doubling the length doubles the resistance, but ρ itself remains unchanged.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A body sinks in water when
options: density of water is greater than density of body
density of water is less than density of body
density of water is equal to density of body
questions.solution:
A body sinks in water when its density is greater than the density of water.

So, the correct answer is:

Option 2: density of water is less than density of body

Reason:

According to the NCERT concept of flotation:

  • If density of body $>$ density of liquid, the body sinks

  • If density of body $<$ density of liquid, the body floats

  • If density of body $=$ density of liquid, the body may remain suspended

Here, for sinking in water:

$$ \text{Density of body} > \text{Density of water} $$

which means

$$ \text{Density of water} < \text{Density of body} $$

Hence, Option 2 is correct.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Bottom of a tank containing water appears to be raised. It is due to
questions.solution:
It is due to Option 2: Refraction of light.

Explanation:

When light rays come from the bottom of the tank and pass from water to air, they bend at the surface because of refraction.

Since the rays bend away from the normal on coming from a denser medium (water) to a rarer medium (air), the bottom appears to be at a smaller depth than its real depth.

So, the bottom of the tank appears raised.

Correct answer:

$$\text{Refraction of light}$$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about speed and velocity is correct?
options: Speed and velocity both are vector quantities.
Speed and velocity both are scalar quantities.
Speed is vector quantity and velocity is scalar quantity.
Speed is scalar quantity and velocity is vector quantity.
questions.solution:
The correct statement about speed and velocity is:

Option D: Speed is a scalar quantity and velocity is a vector quantity.

Here's a detailed explanation:

Speed: Speed is a scalar quantity. This means it only has magnitude and no direction. Speed is the rate at which an object covers distance. For example, if a car travels 60 kilometers per hour, this value indicates how fast the car is moving but does not specify the direction.

Velocity: Velocity, on the other hand, is a vector quantity. This means it includes both magnitude and direction. Velocity is the rate at which an object changes its position. For example, a car moving 60 kilometers per hour to the north has a velocity that specifies both the speed and the direction of movement.
